title: Fields 1: Gravitational and Electric Fields
description: Newton's law of gravitation and Coulomb's law as parallel inverse-square field laws, covering field strength, potential, orbital motion and the analogy between gravitational and electric fields.
objectives: State and apply Newton's law of gravitation and Coulomb's law for point masses and point charges
Define gravitational field strength and electric field strength as force per unit mass/charge, and calculate them for radial and uniform fields
Explain gravitational potential and electric potential and use them to find changes in potential energy
Derive and apply v = sqrt(GM/r) and Kepler's third law (T^2 proportional to r^3) for circular orbits, including geostationary orbits
Compare gravitational and electric fields, explaining why gravitational forces are always attractive while electric forces may attract or repel

lessons.title: Electric fields: point charges and uniform fields
